title: Modern Vertical Slat-Top Console
summary: A console table built by layering flat 2x4s and 1x4s into a solid slab top — no boards run the same direction twice in a row, giving a chunky, striped modern look.
description:
This console's tabletop isn't boards laid side by side — it's built up in alternating layers, 2x4s and 1x4s stacked and glued flat, face to face, so the top reads as a solid slab with a striped edge grain rather than a typical plank top. Lay out the first row (the front-facing layer) with all fasteners driven from the back side, since this is the one row anyone will actually see edge-on.

If you have a pocket-hole jig, attach the top layer to the legs before building up the remaining layers — doing the leg attachment on the first, most accessible layer avoids trying to drive pocket screws into a slab that's already several layers thick. From there, alternate a row of 1x4s against the inside faces of the 2x4s, then a row of 2x4s over those, repeating the pattern until the top reaches the desired width.

**From the second layer onward, nailing and gluing is enough** — the plan is explicit that only the first, visible layer needs pocket-hole precision; everything behind it is structural build-up rather than show face. That's what makes this design fast to repeat: pick a width, keep layering, stop when you get there.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut seven legs (top visible layer) at 56" and 14 build-up layers at 26-1/2" from the 2x4, 8 ft or stud length. Cut twelve alternating layers at 30" from the 1x4. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to ten 8 ft 2x4, 8 ft or stud length and four 8 ft 1x4. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the first (visible) layer
steps.body: Lay out the front-facing legs. If using a pocket-hole jig, attach the top to the legs at this stage while it's still a single accessible layer, driving all fasteners from the back side.

steps.title: Add the first alternating row
steps.body: Layer a row of 1x4s against the inside faces of the 2x4 legs, with glue and nails.

steps.title: Continue building up layers
steps.body: Layer a row of 2x4s over the 1x4s, nailing and gluing. From this point on, nailing and gluing is sufficient — only the first visible layer needed the pocket-hole precision.

steps.title: Finish building and finish
steps.body: Continue alternating 1x4 and 2x4 layers until you reach the desired width. Sand and finish the whole console.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Console table with a built-up slab top from layered 2x4s and 1x4s
